paragraph: Oakland's single-family market is moving with real conviction. Forty-seven homes sold over six months at a median of $699,000, and 61.7 percent of them closed above asking. The sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.04, and the median time on market is 17 days. That's a real seller's market. Homes priced right are drawing multiple offers, and sellers are dictating terms. The price range is broad, from $284,888 to over $2 million, so the market isn't monolithic. A $699,000 home isn't moving the same way as one at $1.67 million, but across the board, sellers have leverage. If you're a buyer in Oakland, you'll need to move fast on homes that fit your criteria and be prepared to compete. If you're selling, pricing competitively and presenting the home well will put you in a strong position, though the best homes are still the ones drawing the serious action.
